title: Stisseria flavicomata
taxonomy: Stisseria flavicomata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The Apocynaceae family is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes the families Loganiaceae, Rubiaceae, and Gelsemiaceae. The order Gentianales belongs to the clade Asterids, within the larger clade Angiosperms. Angiosperms are a group of flowering plants that are a key component of the plant kingdom.

intro: Stisseria flavicomata, also known as Yellow Stisseria or Yellow Borage Vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to South Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and twining habit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stisseria flavicomata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to treat fever, colds, and respiratory infections, as well as being used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and digestive issues.
